intro: Deinandra minthornii, also known as Minthorn's tarweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Deinandra minthornii include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Deinandra minthornii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and ointments, to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
